prevalence.source: ESC Guidelines for the diagnosis and management of atrial fibrillation developed in collaboration with the European Association for Cardio-Thoracic Surgery (EACTS) (2020); European Heart Network
prevalence.trend: Increasing by ~1.5% annually due to population aging

incidence.source: ESC 2020 Guidelines; Global Burden of Disease Study 2019 (via IHME)

burden.source: WHO European Region data; ESC Cardiovascular Disease Statistics 2022

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Detection of irregular heart rhythm without discernible P waves on 12-lead ECG, or irregular RR intervals (ESC 2020 Guidelines, Class I recommendation for ECG confirmation)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Opportunistic screening in high-risk groups (e.g., >65 years or with risk factors) covers ~30-50% in Western EU countries; limited EU-wide programs
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Around 70-80% of eligible patients receive oral anticoagulation for stroke prevention (per ESC Atlas of Cardiology 2023)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Uniformly based on ESC 2020 Guidelines across EU, emphasizing ECG and Holter monitoring
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Integrated approach including rate/rhythm control, anticoagulation (DOACs preferred per ESC), and lifestyle management; catheter ablation for select cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Healthcare disparities between Eastern and Western EU (lower access in some member states); rising burden from aging demographics; anticoagulation underuse in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust cardiology expertise and networks (e.g., EHN initiatives); high adherence to ESC guidelines in countries like Germany and France; EU-funded research on AF management
